blockingIssues.description: Re-review via scrutiny-fixes-m10 resolved the XcmInbox, event-correlator, and market-factory-docs blockers, but LiquidationHookRegistry still stores only one handler per hook type and executeHooks still invokes only that single handler, so the original sequential/all-hooks requirement remains unmet.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile the hook-registry requirement before assigning follow-up fixes. Either LiquidationHookRegistry must support multiple handlers per hook type, or the feature spec and assertion must be explicitly rewritten so workers and reviewers are not operating against conflicting semantics.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: liquidation-hook-registry and VAL-ASYNC-002 require executeHooks to call all registered hooks in sequence, while scrutiny-fixes-m10 says single-handler-per-type is intentional and sufficient.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when hardhat compile may be skipped for narrow follow-up fixes, and require workers to record a deviation whenever compile is omitted.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scrutiny-fixes-m10 omitted `npx hardhat compile` yet marked followedProcedure=true, matching the same skill/procedure mismatch previously seen in event-correlator reviews.
